title: Speech-making Essentials
description: Openings, arcs, pauses, refrains — the five moves of a memorable speech.
summary_pt_br: Cinco movimentos do discurso: hook, promessa, histórias, refrão, chamado à ação. Pausa é pontuação.

scenes.subtitle_en: Openings, arcs, pauses, refrains — the five moves of a memorable speech.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Cinco movimentos do discurso: hook, promessa, histórias, refrão, chamado à ação. Pausa é pontuação.

scenes.text_en: Openings, arcs, pauses, refrains — the five moves of a memorable speech. Focus: speech-making essentials. I stand here not as a teacher, but as a witness.
scenes.text_pt: Cinco movimentos do discurso: hook, promessa, histórias, refrão, chamado à ação. Pausa é pontuação. Foco: speech-making essentials. Estou aqui não como professor, mas como testemunha.

scenes.title: Speech-making Essentials
scenes.body_en: Five moves: HOOK (capture attention), PROMISE (what they'll take away), STORIES (human anchor), REFRAIN (repeated line), CALL (what to do now). Pauses are punctuation.
scenes.body_pt: Cinco movimentos do discurso: hook, promessa, histórias, refrão, chamado à ação. Pausa é pontuação.

examples.en: I stand here not as a teacher, but as a witness.
examples.pt: Estou aqui não como professor, mas como testemunha.

examples.en: I will share three small stories and one big request.
examples.pt: Vou contar três histórias pequenas e um pedido grande.

examples.en: So let us begin — not tomorrow, not Monday, but today.
examples.pt: Então comecemos — não amanhã, não segunda, mas hoje.

exercise.question: Complete: "I stand here not as a ___, but as a witness."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "I stand here not as a teacher, but as a witness."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Estou aqui não como professor, mas como testemunha.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
I will share three small stories and one big request.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "I will share three small stories and one big request."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Vou contar três histórias pequenas e um pedido grande.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Speech-making Essentials — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre speech-making essentials. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Open a three-minute speech on curiosity.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I'm here not because I teach, but because I still learn.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What's your promise to the audience?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Three small stories and one request.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Give me a refrain you'd repeat.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Curiosity is the only luggage that gets lighter.
turns.pt_hint: A única bagagem que fica leve.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I plan three, and hold each two seconds.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Ask one question today you were afraid to ask.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Speeches are architecture. Pauses are the doors.

scenes.title: The graduation speech
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Outline the five moves of a speech. Include at least three speech-making terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: On graduation morning, Kumar practiced his speech in an empty auditorium, stopwatch in hand.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: I stand here not as a teacher, but as a witness to what these students have already become.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: I will share three small stories and one big request before I sit down again.

paragraphs.speaker: Colleague
paragraphs.en: Your refrain — 'curiosity is luggage that gets lighter' — has to repeat at least three times in the speech.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: So let us begin — not tomorrow, not Monday, but today, walking out of this hall together.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Speeches remembered decades later, Kumar believed, always rely on the same five quiet architectural moves.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Speech-making Essentials". Openings, arcs, pauses, refrains — the five moves of a memorable speech. I stand here not as a teacher, but as a witness. I will share three small stories and one big request. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Speech-making Essentials". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.

sources.label: The Economist · Learning English
sources.pt_hint: Jornalismo econômico e político sofisticado — nível C1+.

sources.pt_hint: Ensaios literários e jornalísticos — prosa rica.
